text: Photographs will be telegraphed from any distance. If there be a battle in China a hundred years hence, snapshots of its most striking events will be published in the newspapers an hour later.
author: John Elfreth Watkins Jr.
source: Ladies' Home Journal, 'What May Happen in the Next Hundred Years', 1900

didItHoldUp.analysis: A civil engineer writing in the Ladies' Home Journal in 1900 essentially described photojournalism via the internet — complete with real-time war coverage from the other side of the world. He was off by only one detail: it takes seconds, not an hour. Watkins's full article is astonishing: he also predicted air conditioning, pre-made meals, high-speed trains, and smartphones (described as 'wireless telephone' devices). He may be the most underrated predictor in history.
